headline: Suriani ruling on maternal citizenship rights faces government appeal
context: The landmark 2024 Federal Court ruling granting Malaysian mothers equal rights to confer citizenship on overseas-born children was appealed by the government.

cards.big: Government appeals Suriani ruling on equal maternal citizenship rights
cards.sub: Victory for 1,800 affected mothers now threatened by government challenge

cards.big: Federal Constitution Second Schedule Section 1(b) grants citizenship through fathers only
cards.sub: Court interpreted 'father' to include 'mother' under Article 8 gender equality

cards.big: CEDAW Article 9(2) grants women equal rights with men regarding children's nationality
cards.sub: Malaysia's CEDAW reservation on nationality withdrawn in 2010

cards.big: 1,800 children of Malaysian mothers born overseas affected by constitutional gap
cards.sub: Families separated by citizenship bureaucracy while appeals proceed

cards.big: Appealing gender equality in citizenship rights contradicts constitutional guarantees
Family Frontiers led campaign that generated 56,000 petition signatures by 2024
